name: Monoid & Semigroup
def: A type with an associative binary combine operation; a monoid also has an identity element.
problem: Generalise "combining" (sums, concatenation, merges) so it parallelises and composes uniformly.
mechanism: Define combine: (A, A) → A that is associative (semigroup) plus an identity empty (monoid); laws guarantee safe reassociation and parallel folds.
limits: No HKTs to express it generically in mainstream langs
whenNot: One-off combine on a single concrete type
myths: "Monoid is academic" — every parallel reduce and string concat relies on it

x.formalRole: A semigroup is a set with an associative binary operation •; a monoid adds an identity e with e•a = a•e = a.
x.encoding: No generic Monoid interface across types without HKTs; expressed ad-hoc (reduce + identity, or per-type concat/combine).
x.useCases: Parallel/associative aggregation (sum, max, set-union, string concat), CRDT merges, metric rollups.
misuseRisks: Calling a non-associative op a monoid breaks parallel correctness
